Human.reputation: The default face of civilization across the Realms — humans run most cities, guilds, and thrones, so a human draws little special attention either way.
Human.favorable_with: merchants, city officials, and most common folk who see a human as unremarkable and easy to trust
Human.unfavorable_with: isolationist elves or dwarves who quietly consider humans short-sighted and grasping

Elf.reputation: Graceful, long-lived, and often aloof — respected for wisdom and skill, but sometimes resented for a perceived condescension toward shorter-lived races.
Elf.favorable_with: druids, rangers, scholars, and other elves, who find common ground in patience and reverence for nature or lore
Elf.unfavorable_with: xenophobic rural humans and some dwarven traditionalists carrying old grudges from ancient conflicts

Dwarf.reputation: Blunt, proud, and known for craftsmanship and stubbornness — trusted with a handshake once earned, but slow to give that trust.
Dwarf.favorable_with: guild artisans, miners, and fellow dwarves, who respect plain speech and a job done right
Dwarf.unfavorable_with: some elves due to old grudges, and slick nobles/con-artists who find dwarves impossible to swindle

Halfling.reputation: Small, unassuming, and easy to underestimate — often overlooked in a room, which they've learned to use to their advantage.
Halfling.favorable_with: innkeepers, merchants, and common folk who find halflings disarming and easy to like
Halfling.unfavorable_with: paranoid guards and nobles who've been burned by a halfling's light fingers before

Half-Orc.reputation: Judged on sight by many before they've said a word — assumed brutish or dangerous, which some resent and some learn to use.
Half-Orc.favorable_with: mercenary companies, frontier folk, and those who judge by deeds rather than blood
Half-Orc.unfavorable_with: city guards quick to suspicion, and prejudiced nobles or merchants who won't hide their unease

Tiefling.reputation: Infernal blood shows in horns or eyes that unsettle the superstitious — treated with suspicion in devout or rural areas, more readily accepted in cosmopolitan cities.
Tiefling.favorable_with: cosmopolitan city-dwellers, rogues' guilds, and other outcasts who don't judge by appearance
Tiefling.unfavorable_with: devout temple-goers and superstitious villagers who mutter about ill omens

Dragonborn.reputation: Rare enough in most regions to draw stares and whispers — respected for evident strength and draconic heritage, but rarely well understood by locals.
Dragonborn.favorable_with: soldiers, warriors, and those who respect martial bearing and honor
Dragonborn.unfavorable_with: superstitious rural folk unsettled by their draconic appearance and unfamiliar customs

Gnome.reputation: Curious, quick-witted, and often assumed to be tinkering with something — welcomed by those who value cleverness, dismissed as odd by the humorless.
Gnome.favorable_with: artificers, scholars, and merchants who appreciate a clever mind and quick tongue
Gnome.unfavorable_with: rigid traditionalists and military types impatient with gnomish tangents and gadgetry

Fighter.reputation: A plain, trusted martial profession — city guards and soldiers see a fighter as one of their own.
Fighter.favorable_with: city watch, mercenary companies, and soldiers who respect trained steel
Fighter.unfavorable_with: criminal elements wary of someone who can actually fight back

Wizard.reputation: Arcane learning draws both respect and unease — scholars admire it, common folk often fear what they don't understand.
Wizard.favorable_with: scholars, sages, and other arcane practitioners eager to talk shop
Wizard.unfavorable_with: superstitious commoners and some temples wary of unregulated arcane power

Rogue.reputation: Assumed to have sticky fingers whether deserved or not — merchants watch their purses, but the criminal underworld sees a potential peer.
Rogue.favorable_with: thieves' guilds, fences, and information brokers who speak the same language
Rogue.unfavorable_with: merchants, nobles, and guards who assume the worst on sight

Cleric.reputation: A visible servant of the divine — treated with the respect (or wariness) owed to their faith's local standing.
Cleric.favorable_with: temple-goers, the faithful, and those in need of healing or blessing
Cleric.unfavorable_with: rival faiths' devotees, or the irreligious who find divine authority overbearing

Ranger.reputation: At home in the wild more than the city — trusted as a guide, viewed as an outsider by court society.
Ranger.favorable_with: hunters, druids, foresters, and rural folk who value wilderness know-how
Ranger.unfavorable_with: city nobles who find rangers uncouth and unpolished

Bard.reputation: Charming and quick with words — welcomed in taverns and courts alike, but not always taken seriously.
Bard.favorable_with: innkeepers, performers, and nobles fond of entertainment and gossip
Bard.unfavorable_with: stern military types who see bards as unserious distractions

Paladin.reputation: A visible symbol of a sworn oath — inspires trust among the devout and law-abiding, and discomfort among those with something to hide.
Paladin.favorable_with: temple-goers, the lawful, and those seeking a champion for their cause
Paladin.unfavorable_with: criminals, cultists, and anyone currently breaking a law the paladin might notice

Warlock.reputation: Power from an unseen patron unsettles nearly everyone who learns of it — kept quiet by the wise, feared when discovered.
Warlock.favorable_with: occultists, fellow warlocks, and those desperate enough to seek forbidden power themselves
Warlock.unfavorable_with: temples, paladins, and anyone suspicious of pacts with otherworldly beings

Barbarian.reputation: Untamed strength from beyond the city walls — respected in a fight, viewed as uncivilized in a drawing room.
Barbarian.favorable_with: frontier folk, mercenaries, and those who value raw strength and honesty
Barbarian.unfavorable_with: courtly nobles who find barbarian bluntness embarrassing or crude

Druid.reputation: A voice for nature in a world of cities — deeply respected in rural and wild communities, alien to urban sensibilities.
Druid.favorable_with: rural folk, rangers, and other druids who share reverence for the natural order
Druid.unfavorable_with: industrious merchants and nobles whose interests often conflict with nature's

Sorcerer.reputation: Magic in the blood rather than from study — treated much like a wizard by most, though some see innate power as less trustworthy than learned discipline.
Sorcerer.favorable_with: arcane scholars curious about their bloodline, and fellow sorcerers
Sorcerer.unfavorable_with: traditionalist wizards who consider innate magic undisciplined, and superstitious commoners

Monk.reputation: Disciplined and unarmed but clearly dangerous — respected by those who value self-mastery, misunderstood by those expecting a sword.
Monk.favorable_with: monasteries, disciplined soldiers, and those who respect quiet self-control
Monk.unfavorable_with: brash duelists who underestimate an unarmed opponent, to their later regret

default_reaction.reputation: An uncommon sight in these lands — most locals aren't quite sure what to make of them, reacting with curiosity more than judgment.
default_reaction.unfavorable_with: the deeply traditional and the easily unsettled by the unfamiliar
